item.title: SNB Is About to Reveal Its Resolve on Franc Strength
item.description: The Swiss National Bank’s determination to cap gains in the franc will draw the spotlight in a decision that is likely to avoid the more drastic option of negative borrowing costs.

item.title: China’s AI Stocks Rise as Nvidia’s Huang Calls OpenClaw 'the Next ChatGPT'
item.description: Chinese shares related to OpenClaw jumped, buoyed by bullish comments from Nvidia Corp.’s chief on the potential of artificial intelligence agents.

item.title: Stada Hunts for Major Consumer Health Deal After Record Profit
item.description: German pharmaceutical firm Stada said it’s ready to make a major consumer health acquisition as it books record profit and transitions to new ownership.

item.title: Ghana Rate Cut Still Expected Despite Iran War
item.description: Ghana’s interest-rate easing cycle is expected to continue on Wednesday despite inflation risks stemming from conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Alibaba Hikes AI Computing Prices Up to 34% After Demand Soars
item.description: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. is raising prices for its AI computing and storage products by as much as 34%, responding to fast-growing demand and rising infrastructure costs.

item.title: Geely Profit Beats Estimates as Sales Approach Those of BYD
item.description: Geely Automobile Holdings Ltd.’s full-year profit beat analyst estimates and climbed to a record as China’s second-largest carmaker closed in on market leader BYD Co.’s sales, thanks to the popularity of its EX2 hatchbacks and Zeekr sport utility vehicles.

item.title: Soy Futures Drop on Delayed Trump-Xi Meeting, Oil Price Decline
item.description: US soy futures dropped after President Donald Trump confirmed he would be postponing his trip to China for trade talks, while a decline in crude oil also weighed.

item.title: Bonanza or Bubble? Where AI Goes From Here
item.description: AI is now coding apps, drafting contracts and organizing marketing campaigns. Yet the money being spent on the technology has ballooned into a vast liability hanging over financial markets, and it's still not clear how it will all pay off

item.title: Investors Can Handle What’s Happening in Hormuz
item.description: Earnings forecasts are still holding up share prices despite war’s surprises.

item.title: Cash Crunch Drives Nigerian Power Producers Out of Business
item.description: A number of heavily indebted Nigerian power producers have ceased operating because their customers aren’t settling their bills and they can’t afford to run their equipment or pay for gas supplies.

item.title: Singapore’s Largest Taxi Firm Raises Fares as Fuel Prices Climb
item.description: ComfortDelGro Corp., Singapore’s largest taxi operator, will raise fares temporarily as it moves to shield cabbies from surging fuel costs driven by a global energy shock.

item.title: Indonesian Rate Cut Cycle Likely Over Due to War, Economists Say
item.description: Bank Indonesia’s monetary easing cycle has likely come to an end, economists say, scrapping earlier calls of as many as two more interest-rate cuts this year.

item.title: Samsung Weighs Multi-Year Deals to Ease Memory Chip Crunch Fears
item.description: Samsung Electronics Co. is considering a shift toward multi-year contracts for memory chips, a much longer timeframe than is typical that may help stabilize supply and ease concerns about a shortage of the essential components.

item.title: Philippines in Talks With China and Russia on Fertilizer Supply
item.description: The Philippines is in discussions with major fertilizer producers including China and Russia to safeguard supply on fears exporters won’t deliver on contracts as the Middle East war keeps prices high.

item.title: Soaring Crude, Weak Rupee Put Pressure on India’s Forex Reserves
item.description: The RBI is having to to step in more often of late to support the rupee.

item.title: Sunway Healthcare Soars After Biggest Malaysia IPO in Nine Years
item.description: Shares of Malaysia’s Sunway Healthcare Bhd. jumped nearly 32% in their debut after the company raised 2.86 billion ringgit ($731 million) in the country’s largest initial public offering in nine years.

item.title: RBA’s Rate-Hike Split Raises Questions Over Timing of Tightening
item.description: Economists and traders are at odds over whether Australia’s central bank will deliver another interest-rate increase in May or wait for the fallout from the Iran war, as a split board weakens conviction for a third consecutive hike.

item.title: Libya Redirects Oil Flows After Sharara Pipeline Leak and Fire
item.description: Libya’s National Oil Corp. reported a fire after a pipeline leak from the country’s largest oil field, according to a statement.

item.title: PetroChina Keeps Downstream Gas Offers Stable Despite War Risks
item.description: PetroChina Co., the country’s biggest natural gas supplier, will keep its contract prices largely unchanged this year to shield industrial consumers from surging global energy prices due to the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Hong Kong Regulatory Scrutiny Puts Share Sale Boom at Risk
item.description: Intensifying regulatory scrutiny is rattling Hong Kong’s financial industry and raising the prospects of a slowdown in booming share sales in Asia’s premier fundraising hub.

item.title: Apollo Makes First Hire for Singapore’s $1 Billion Private Fund
item.description: Apollo Global Management has made its first dedicated hire for a $1 billion private credit fund aimed at Singapore’s high-growth enterprises, people familiar with the matter said, as the city-state seeks to strengthen its foothold in the asset class.

item.title: Data Center Firm DayOne Seeks to Boost Loan to Record $7 Billion
item.description: DayOne Data Centers Ltd. is seeking to double the size of an existing loan to as much as $7 billion, according to people familiar with the matter, in what would be the largest borrowing for the sector by any firm in Asia.

item.title: Ringgit Hits Strongest Level Versus Singapore Dollar Since 2021
item.description: Malaysia’s ringgit rose to the strongest versus the Singapore dollar in five years as higher energy prices support the net energy exporter, which was also propelled by optimism over artificial intelligence.

item.title: Elliott Takes ‘Significant’ Stake in Mitsui OSK Lines: Reuters
item.description: Elliott Investment Management LP has taken a significant stake in Japanese shipping company Mitsui OSK Lines Ltd., Reuters reported, citing two people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Taiwan Insurers Cut Forwards by Record After Regulatory Changes
item.description: Taiwan’s life insurers have slashed their holdings of forwards at a record pace after regulatory changes allowed them to spread out the impact of currency fluctuations on their balance sheets.

item.title: Aluminum Piles Up in China as Iran War Shrinks Global Supplies
item.description: Aluminum, one of the metals most directly affected by the war in Iran, is struggling to attract buyers in China after prices surged to a four-year high.

item.title: SMBC Asks Banks to Confirm Saudi Energy Loan Backing Amid War
item.description: Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corp. has reached out to Asian banks backing an around $1.5 billion Saudi loan deal to reconfirm their commitments, an unusual step highlighting the risks the conflict in Iran poses for lending in the region.

item.title: Private Markets Chiefs Quit Australia’s Sovereign Wealth Fund
item.description: Two of the most senior private markets executives at Australia’s Future Fund have quit the A$267 billion ($190 billion) sovereign wealth fund.

item.title: Japan’s Export Growth Slows on China Holidays, US Tariffs Impact
item.description: Japan’s exports grew at a slower pace, as tariffs weighed on car shipments to the US fell and as demand in China slumped due to the Lunar New Year holidays.

item.title: Trump Officials Fire Back at Anthropic Over AI Tool Ban
item.description: The Trump administration vowed a legal fight to oust Anthropic PBC from all US government agencies following a dispute over how the company’s artificial intelligence technology would be used.
